title: Nursery Rhyme. DL. Natural History.
content:
The winds they did blow,
            The leaves they did wag;
        Along came a beggar boy,
            And put me in his bag.

        He took me up to London,
            A lady did me buy,
        Put me in a silver cage,
            And hung me up on high.

        With apples by the fire,
            And nuts for to crack,
        Besides a little feather bed
            To rest my little back.
meaning:
This evocative piece by Unknown, titled "Nursery Rhyme. DL. Natural History.", represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
